feed.title: Benjamin Kan
feed.subtitle: Project portfolio and blog of Benjamin Kan.

entry.title: Record Your Terminal With Terminalizer
entry.summary: Did you ever want to demo a terminal program or remotely explain a sequence of commands to a friend or colleague? If so, then terminalizer is a tool for you… Example recording with terminalizer Installation While the installation steps described in the docs are not very detailed. When simply followed them, I ended up with errors. It turned out that Node.js 16 is not yet supported. Consequen...

entry.title: Quick Data Visualization in Python
entry.summary: In in previous post, I wrote about Matplotlib as a must have tool on your system to do interactive data visualization in an instant. Today, I worked on a playground project called b3nk4n/quick-data-visualization where I started to collect and compare a few Python plotting libraries using a small set of data, such a nested Python list, a time-series in form of a NumPy array and a Pandas datafr...

entry.title: Retrospective on 6 Years of Windows App Development

link.title: Retrospective on 6 Years of Windows App Development

entry.summary: This article is a follow-up on previous posts about reaching individual milestones on my journey as an Indie Window App Developer: 100k Downloads in Windows Phone Store — 05/2013 250k Downloads in Windows Phone Store — 10/2013 500k Downloads in Windows Phone Store — 09/2014 1 Millionen Downloads in Windows App Store — 05/2015 Intro About 5 years have passed in the meantime after I ...

entry.title: Sharing Command-line Options in Python Argparse

link.title: Sharing Command-line Options in Python Argparse

entry.summary: This is probably no big news that argparse makes it easy to write user-friendly command-line interfaces. It comes out-of-the-box and is therefore the standard tool if you create a CLI tool in Python. Basic usage of argparse The following snippet shows a basic usage example of argparse with a single param argument that can be passed to the command line. import argparse def main(argv): prin...

entry.title: Manage Your Java Environment
entry.summary: In case you are familiar with Pyhon, I guess you also have heard of virtualenv. This is a tool to create isolated Python environments, which lets you easily create or switch projects between Python 2 and Python 3. In Java, there are also different version out there. While version 7 or 8 are probably still most commonly used in production, even newer versions like 10 (current default of Ubuntu ...
